heroHeadline: RUTTE COMPLETES ANKARA VISIT; GERMANY UNVEILS FIRST-EVER MILITARY STRATEGY; EU TESTS ARTICLE 42.7 AS NATO FALLBACK
heroSubtitle: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte completed his two-day Ankara visit on April 22, meeting President Erdoğan and visiting ASELSAN — pledging NATO 'will always defend' Turkey as Iran's missiles entered Turkish airspace four times. Erdoğan offered to revive Russia-Ukraine negotiations. Rutte called for joint NATO defense production. Simultaneously, Germany's Defense Minister Pistorius unveiled Germany's first-ever formally adopted military strategy — 'Responsibility for Europe' — targeting expansion of the Bundeswehr from 185,000 to 260,000 troops to become Europe's strongest conventional army. A NATO nuclear deterrence symposium concluded in Istanbul on April 22. On April 23, the Washington Post reported the EU is crisis-testing Article 42.7 of the EU Treaty — its mutual defense clause — as a contingency for Article 5 unreliability, with officials now convinced Trump's security priorities 'lie elsewhere.' The July 7–8 Ankara NATO Summit remains the next institutional anchor for the alliance's deepest crisis since its founding.
footerNote: Data sourced from NATO HQ, White House, US Department of Defense, Reuters, Bloomberg, Wall Street Journal, The Economist, The Telegraph, TIME, Politico, German Federal Foreign Office, Axios, NPR, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, EU News, Swedish MFA, France 24, Euronews, CNN, PBS NewsHour, Gulf News, US Senate Foreign Relations Committee, GB News, Welt am Sonntag, Turkish Minute, CFR, Stars and Stripes
